nodes.name: Mentawai malkoha
nodes.summary: Mentawai malkoha（Phaenicophaeus oeneicaudus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Mentawai malkoha。

nodes.name: Mentawai malkoha
nodes.summary: The Mentawai malkoha (Phaenicophaeus oeneicaudus) is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae. It is endemic to the Mentawai Islands off the west of Sumatra. It was formerly considered to be conspecific with the chestnut-breasted malkoha. Taxonomy The Mentawai malkoha was formally described in 1855 by the French naturalists Jules and Édouard Verreaux under the current binomial name Phaenicophaeus oeneicaudus. They believed that their specimen had come from Ceylon (now Sri Lanka). The Italian zoologist Tommaso Salvadori realised that this was an error and the specimen must instead have come from the Mentawai Islands which lie in the Indian Ocean approximately 150 km (93 mi) off the western coast of Sumatra. The genus name Phaenicophaeus is from Ancient Greek φοινικοφαης (phoinikophaēs) meaning "of crimson appearance" or "red-gleaming". The specific epithet oeneicaudus combines the Latin oenoo- meaning "wine" and cauda meaning "tail". The Mentawai malkoha was formerly treated as a subspecies of the chestnut-breasted malkoha (Phaenicophaeus curvirostris). It was promoted to species status based on the morphological differences. References
